A delegation from Masdar visits Al Shera’a, DEWA’s new headquarters in Al Jaddaf, which is the world’s tallest, largest and smartest net-positive government building. The visit included a meeting between saeed Mohammed Al Tayer, MD & CEO of DEWA, and Mohamed Jameel Al Ramahi, CEO of Masdar (Abu Dhabi Future Energy Company).

Maktoum bin Mohammed: I reviewed the Ministry of Finance’s 2025 Annual Report, which documents a year of achievements and a continued journey of advancing fiscal policies, strengthening sustainability, and reinforcing the UAE’s global competitiveness. The report reflects an exceptional year marked by strategic steps to enhance the efficiency of government spending and further develop the tax system, alongside the execution of nine Islamic Treasury Sukuk auctions with a total value of AED 9.9 billion. It also highlights the launch of the “UAE Global Cadres” initiative, designed to empower Emirati youth to pursue careers in regional and international financial institutions and organisations. I

Summary of incidents in the South Sharon area MDA paramedics and EMTs pronounced the death of a man approximately 35 years old with gunshot wounds, and treated and evacuated to Meir and Beilinson hospitals five injured casualties, two in serious condition and three moderate.

Dubai’s Roads and Transport Authority in Collaboration with Shamal Holding, completes 90% of a project to construct a 1,500-metre bridge with two lanes in each direction, providing direct entry and exit between Sheikh Zayed Road and Dubai Harbour. The bridge will have a total capacity of up to 6,000 vehicles per hour in both directions. @rta_dubai
